title: dactylis glomerata f. vivipara
intro: Dactylis glomerata f. vivipara, also known as viviparous bentgrass or viviparous grass, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant is a sterile hybrid of Dactylis glomerata and Dactylis hispida, and it is known for its unique ability to produce seeds without fertilization. The medicinal properties of Dactylis glomerata f. vivipara include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin irritations, and its health benefits include soothing coughs, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing.
